title: Tach Guard Diff
description: The classified difference between a guard session's baseline (taken at `begin`) and the current working tree (`tach guard diff --json`). `added`/`modified`/`deleted` are the raw change sets; every changed path is then partitioned into `in_scope` or `out_of_scope` against the goal's fs.write authority. `rejected` is true when any path is out of scope — the same condition that blocks `verify` and `commit`. `blind_spots` lists the ignore patterns in effect (frozen at begin), so the gate's uncovered areas are explicit.

added.description: Repo-relative paths present in the tree but not the baseline.

modified.description: Paths whose content, kind, executable bit, or symlink target changed.

deleted.description: Paths present in the baseline but gone from the tree.

in_scope.description: Changed paths that fall inside the goal's fs.write scope.

out_of_scope.description: Changed paths outside the goal's fs.write scope — these block commit.

rejected.description: True when at least one changed file is out of scope.

blind_spots.description: Ignore patterns in effect for this run (frozen at begin) — conventional build/dependency roots the gate does not watch.
